threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'hello' to the bus driver. The rest of us just nod at our phones now.
Caught a night bus last week; the driver stopped to hand a lost phone to a kid running from a kebab shop—no one else even noticed.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using empty beer bottles as bowling pins in the street, I knew I was really a Neuköllner.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a döner kebab at 4 AM after a night out and hear live music wafting from the Spree's riverside clubs.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re from here when they flinch at the Muni delays but keep their lunch plans at La Taqueria, no matter what. It's all part of the game.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a dog commandeering the tracks at Civic Center today—Muni stood still while passengers debated who would break the news to its owner.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every plaza is for strolling; locals know they're for gathering, gossiping, and playing petanca. It's a whole vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment when they encounter a packed train during the Rodalies strikes. It's like watching a deer in headlights.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 'Overground' is an extension of the Tube instead of a completely different world. Welcome to Hackney!
Last week, the 196 was packed with a bunch of teenagers debating the best chicken shops in Brixton. You could smell the spicy scents wafting from their bags.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I heard that random jam session on the corner of Landstraße and Flughafenstraße at 3 AM, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find a party that starts at a U-Bahn station after a transit strike, with DJs spinning amidst confused commuters.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone's from here when they instinctively swerve to avoid the giant pothole on 24th that’s been there for years. Tourists just plow right in.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo calmly pulling an inflatable flamingo onto the bus. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all hipster cafes, but many locals still get their bread from the old-school panaderies that have been here for decades.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones who still say 'bon dia' instead of just 'hola' when entering a bar. The locals barely raise an eyebrow anymore.
